entry.title: Account Assignment in TRM Financial Transactions - WBS Element
entry.content:
This article describes how to use WBS Element in financial transactions in Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) of in S/4HANA Public Cloud, mainly focus on using WBS element in account assignments which will influence the posting journals arising from TRM transactions.

1. When creating transactions for certain product categories (600, 760, 620), a WBS element can be entered in sub-area Additional Fields of Administr. tab.

For example, below is a FX forward transaction (product category 600):

When doing so in the transactions of product category 600 or 760 (OTC FX option), the major purpose is using WBS element as a differentiation criterion in hedge management to match the FX exposure and FX derivative at the level of WBS element. For more details regarding differentiation criteria in hedge management, please refer to help document https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e5ec5859d8e54df98492d80564a734c0/618b3d58eaa89144e10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US.

Unlike cost center, the column of WBS element is unavailable in position indicator by default, unless that WBS element is enabled as a differentiation term through configuration activity 104115 Define and Assign Differentiations (which will be introduced later). That means, WBS element can't even be manually entered in the Position Indicator of TRM transactions, and consequently, the WBS element (entered in sub-area Additional Fields of Administr. tab) will NOT be carried over to the posting journals generated from the transaction. In a word, it has no impact on postings.

2. Through the app Additional Account Assignments - Substitution Rules, you can create a substitution rule for WBS Element from TRM side.

This app provides comprehensive preconditions (characteristics from TRM transactions) which can help to determine the WBS element based on complicated combinations of multiple characteristics from TRM transactions.

Just one simple example:

The substitution rule defined in this app take effect when TRM is transferring data to accounting interface, therefore, the determined WBS element is not reflected in any part of the TRM transactions.

Usually, the determined WBS element via this app will apply to journal items which are posted with primary cost element that requires cost object like WBS element, cost center. If both of cost center and WBS element are determined for the same journal item, then the actual posting will be recorded in WBS element, and cost center will be the role of statistical posting.

For more details of this app, please refer to help document https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e5ec5859d8e54df98492d80564a734c0/b06cfc3ba30a46809a181952bb6b78f4.html?locale=en-US.

3. Through configuration activity 104115 (Define and Assign Differentiations), you can define a differentiation which includes term WBS Element, and then assign this differentiation to proper valuation area(s) and/or accounting code(s).

Afterwards, when creating a financial transaction within the related accounting code or for related valuation area, in the tab Administr., you will see the field WBS Element within the subarea Position Assignment. And this WBS Element field is mandatory to be entered with a value.

The WBS Element entered in sub-area Position Assignment of Administr. tab is stored in the position indicator of this transaction. In the position indicator, the WBS Element column is grayed out, because it must be identical with the WBS Element specified in sub-area Position Assignment of Administr. tab.

Consequently, the WBS Element stored in position indicator will be carried over to posting journal items generated from this transaction, and the WBS element will apply to the journal items which are posted with primary cost element that requires cost object like WBS element, cost center. If both of cost center and WBS element are determined for the same journal item, then the actual posting will be recorded in WBS element, and cost center will be the role of statistical posting.

As mentioned, the WBS element field is available by default in the subarea Additional Field in tab Administr. of the transactions of certain product categories 600, 760, 620. For the transactions of a product category other than 600, 760, 620, you can enable WBS element as a differentiation via Define and Assign Differentiations, then WBS element field will be available in the subarea Position Assignment in tab Administr.. This method can be useful when requiring separate and manual determination of the WBS element and store it in tab Administr. of the transaction, and this WBS element should be used to record the profit/loss arising from this transaction from accounting perspective.

The important thing is that the decision to use Define and Assign Differentiations should be made cautiously, as it not only makes the WBS element field visible in transactions but also has several significant impacts on transaction processing and accounting, which need to be thoroughly evaluated before configuration (not limited to following points):

  • For every transaction within the valuation area and/or accounting code which is assigned with this differentiation, WBS element field will be a mandatory field to entered in financial transactions.
  • Because of the above point, the intercompany trading (e.g. transaction mirroring for intercompany investment and borrowing) won’t function properly, because WBS element will be the mandatory field for the mirrored transaction, but it can’t be automatically derived.
  • Position and quantity of financial transactions will be split according to WBS elements in addition to predefined differentiations, this effect is especially obvious for financial transactions and class positions of securities (e.g. bond, stock).
  • Because of above point, the profit/loss (for example, realized gain/loss when derecognition) of securities class positions will be measured and accounted in line with each WBS element.

And please also note the limitations of Define and Assign Differentiations:

  • Once there are business transactions for a valuation area and/or an accounting code, it is no longer possible to assign a differentiation to that valuation area and/or accounting code in the same tenant.
  • When there are transactional data in production tenant, even if it is possible to assign differentiation in customizing tenant, transporting this customizing to production system will be very critical for position management in production system.
  • Once the configuration is done in Define and Assign Differentiations and there are already business transactions, it is not recommended that the assignment of differentiation is changed or deleted, as it is very critical for position management.

Therefore, it is strongly recommended that you first understand the implications of the configuration of Define and Assign Differentiations at the beginning of the implementation project of TRM. If possible, thoroughly tests in a reference system, such as a starter system, is recommended. Only after this should you proceed to configure it in the official customizing tenant, conduct comprehensive testing in the test tenant before transporting the configuration to the production tenant.

entry.title: Unlocking the Power of SAP Central Business Configuration for Cloud Solutions through Scoping
entry.content:
A scenario consists of predefined content that facilitates the implementation of a business process. When defining your scope, you select which available scenarios you wish to include in your solution.

For further details about a specific scenario, click on its title in a CBC Workspace -> Scoping to access a more comprehensive description.

Use the following URL to check the available business process for - SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

To verify the availability of scope items for S/4HANA Cloud, consult the Central Business Configuration - Road Map Viewer via SAP for Me and download the Availability and Dependencies of Scope Items.xlsx (SAP Customer).

Download the Activity List and search for the relevant scope item to check if it requires additional licenses for activation, and identify whether it is categorized as Non-standard, Default, or Optional. Additionally, confirm that the scope item is accessible for the targeted countries by examining column S - BY in the Excel file.

Default scope items can be incorporated through the "Define Scope" activity. To do so, open your project, navigate to Scoping, and search for the desired scope item, then click "Add." The "Add" button will be visible once you have opened the "Define Scope" activity.

Example - The scope 2QW is added

After adding the scope item, confirm your selection by clicking the "My Selected Scope" button located at the top right corner of the project. You will be able to view the added scope item.

Checking the added scope through - My Selected Scope

If a Default scope item does not appear in your search within the "Define Scope" activity, verify its availability for the scoped countries through the excel file or SAP Signavio Process Navigator.

Note that certain scope items are only available during the "Initial Scoping" phase, which occurs prior to project deployment.

Checkout the below KBA to find the list of scope item's which can be added only during initial scoping phase:

3425018 - CBC: How to check whether a scope item is only available for initial scoping - SAP for Me

Additionally follow the KBA to know more about how to use the Availability dependencies list - 3539934 - How-to use the "Availability and dependencies of scope items list" when activating Scope Items - SAP for Me.

If the required scope item is Non-standard and requires a license, refer to the KBA - 3122430 - Scope item activation request for SAP Central Business Configuration provisioned S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me.

entry.title: Solution Order Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition kicks off 2025 with a new era of business-empowering, productivity-enhancing and process optimizing innovation – to hear about some of the highlights across the solution, check this video out. As a key part of the Public Cloud ERP, Solution Order Management again delivers new capabilities that help drive increased revenues and growth with optimized solution-based business processes. In this blog, we’ll take a look at the new features being delivered with the 2502 release.

For a brief summary of my top two highlights, take a look at the below video:


Flexible Pricing for Solution Bundles (6BU)

Obtaining higher revenues and growth with solution-based business models results from the ability to combine a range of different types of products in a solution which can be customized and adapted to suit customer needs. A larger scope of more varied offerings could require a high degree of manual input and effort in creating solution orders, so in 2024, we rolled out Advanced Solution Bundling functionality. In a nutshell, solution bundles are pre-configured products consisting of multiple components  packaged together and sold as part of a solution order. To make selling complex & customizable solutions even more efficient, solution bundles are pre-defined and configured in the product master data and can subsequently be added to a solution order, saving solution order specialists time and effort in bundling items. Find out more in this blog post. Revisiting solution bundles in 2502, we have updated them to enable the inclusion of one-time service products which previously weren’t supported with the initial scope.

Notably, with 2502 we have enhanced pricing capabilities to provide greater flexibility to sell items with bundle-specific pricing. Firstly, we have introduced two new price conditions for assigning discounts to solution orders at header level. These define an overall percentage discount based on either the gross amount (price condition type DRG1) or Net amount (price condition type DRN1) of the solution order. The system automatically applies this discount item level, and the discount is applied to all items included in a solution order bundle.

Additionally, we have introduced dedicated pricing for sub-item components within a bundle, increasing flexibility when selling products as part of a bundle or as part of a sales bill of material (BOM) within a bundle. Reflecting whether an item is included in a bundle, this new capability enables you to sell an item at a specific price instead of the standard product price. To support this, new price condition types are now available for solution orders, which can be configured and adjusted in the Manage Prices app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

  • PSBP (Bundle Price) – For bundle sub-items that are charged as a one-time fee, such as sales and service items.
  • PSMB (Monthly Bundle Price) – For bundle sub-items that are billed periodically, such as service contract items.
  • PSSK (Sales BOM Price) – For sub-items that are part of a sales BOM within a solution bundle.

When an item is added to a solution order as part of a bundle or sales kit, the system automatically determines its price, and the corresponding price condition type is displayed under the price details tab in the item view. Once the solution order is released, the pricing details are carried forward to all follow-up transactions, ensuring a seamless process and reducing manual effort and input errors when handling complex solution bundles with varied pricing structures.

This feature provides greater flexibility in defining pricing strategies for solution selling and enables businesses to offer a broader and more differentiated portfolio of solution orders, ultimately helping to improve business margins. To ensure the new price condition types are available, make sure to update your reference content to the latest version.

Take an in-depth look at the new pricing parameters for solution bundles in the below demo.



Subscription Billing Enhancements (4GT)

Next, we’ll explore the integration between Solution Order Management and SAP Subscription Billing, where we’ve introduced several key enhancements with the 2502 release. These updates are designed to improve the efficiency of creating, modifying, and managing subscription products within solution orders.

With this release, additional fields have been introduced in Solution Order Management to provide greater flexibility and control over subscription-based offerings. When opening the details view of a subscription billing item in a solution order, you will now see a new checkbox named "Create Subscription as Pending." This feature allows the solution order specialist to control the exact timing of subscription activation. If the checkbox is left unchecked, the original activation setting from SAP Subscription Billing will remain in effect, ensuring seamless integration with the existing configuration. However, if the checkbox is selected, the specialist can override the default setting, providing more control over subscription lifecycle management.

Additionally, a new header section for "Subscription Terms" has been introduced. This section enables users to adapt and modify the default subscription terms defined in SAP Subscription Billing directly within Solution Order Management. By doing so, businesses can manage subscription conditions more effectively and govern the entire lifecycle of follow-up subscriptions without requiring separate modifications in the billing system.

 New fields have also been introduced within the billing section, to further enhance flexibility in subscription sales. These include:

  • Billing Cycle and Billing Cycle Reference Date, which help determine how and when the system schedules billing, allowing for better alignment with business requirements.
  • Additional fields that provide the option to define whether recurring charges should be billed together or waived, giving businesses more options when structuring subscription pricing models.

 Several further fields that were previously only available in SAP Subscription Billing are now reflected in Solution Order Management. These include:

  • Withdraw Period End Date
  • Minimum Term End Date
  • Expected Term End Date
  • Expected Term Extension
  • Subscription Pricing Date
  • Waiver Period Number
  • Waiver Period Number Unit

These fields will be available across multiple touchpoints, including the UI, API, and, where applicable, in output management and the migration object of the Solution Order.

Additionally, the UI of the Subscription Item has been redesigned to provide a more intuitive user experience. Enhancements include:

  • Two new assignment groups for terms and billing information, allowing for a more structured and logical organization of subscription data.
  • A more meaningful assignment of both existing and new subscription fields to their respective assignment groups.
  • Improved presentation of negative charges, making it easier to identify and interpret these amounts.
  • Better visualization of recurring prices, ensuring greater clarity in pricing details.

Finally, to round out these improvements, we have added a new column in the Price Calculation View table called "Recurrence." This column indicates the frequency at which recurring charges are billed, making it easier for users to track and manage subscription-based pricing directly from the solution order interface.

With these enhancements, Solution Order Management now offers greater flexibility and control over subscription-based sales, improving efficiency and reducing manual effort when managing complex billing structures. The improved UI and expanded field availability ensure a more streamlined and user-friendly approach to subscription pricing and lifecycle management.

Click the video below to watch an overview of the new Subscription Billing features delivered with 2502:

 New API v4 for Solution Orders(4GT)


SAP S/4HANA Cloud has taken a major step forward in solution order management with the introduction of a new OData V4-based API. This enhancement brings improved performance, efficiency, and integration capabilities, making it easier than ever to manage solution orders effectively.

One of the biggest advantages of this new API is its optimized communication efficiency. By leveraging OData V4, it reduces data transfer payloads and enhances request handling, resulting in faster processing times and lower resource consumption. This translates to quicker response times and a more streamlined user experience.

Additionally, the API supports full CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) functionality, allowing users to efficiently manage solution order items. While individual items within an order can be modified or removed, solution orders themselves remain protected from deletion, ensuring data integrity and maintaining order history.



Contract Accounts in Solution Orders (4GT)

A key new feature introduced in the 2502 release is the support for FI-CA contract accounts in Solution Orders. FI-CA (Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a specialized subledger designed for high-volume billing, invoicing, and receivables management.

With the integration of contract accounts into solution orders, the system retrieves contract account details from the defined payer role and posts the billing information to the FI-CA ledger for further processing. When a payer has a contract account assigned, the system automatically selects it; however, if multiple contract accounts exist for the payer, a popup screen allows users to choose the relevant account from a list.

Contract accounts are maintained both at the header and item levels of the solution order, supporting sales and subscription items. In CFD1 release 1 of 2502, this scope will expand to include service contract items. Notably, different contract accounts can be defined at the header and item levels, allowing for different contract accounts for each solution in cases where multiple solutions are sold in a single order.

When the solution order is released, the specified contract account information is transferred to follow-up documents, which are then processed for billing. Once invoicing is completed, the document is posted to the FI-CA subledger for accounts receivable and payable. From there, users can leverage central contract accounting functions for payment handling, collection management, or clearing open items. The integration of contract accounts into solution orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud enhances financial management by enabling precise billing, streamlined payments, and improved oversight through the FI-CA component.

This enhancement centralizes all financial transactions related to solution orders under a single contract account, simplifying tracking and reporting. It also automates billing, improving accuracy, reducing manual effort, and helping prevent invoicing discrepancies. Additionally, supporting contract accounts provides a comprehensive view of customer transactions, speeding up the resolution of any billing or payment queries.

Feedback of Reference Equipment for Service Order Items (4GT)

A central use case of solution order is selling related products under a single order efficiently and with minimal manual input. Supporting this, in 2024 we introduced equipment linkage for service contract items to be associated with a physical good in a solution order. With 2502, we expand this functionality to support non-recurring service items to be linked.

At its core, equipment linkage ensures that when the post goods issue is processed for a sales item, the equipment ID and its corresponding serial number are automatically transferred to the associated service. This is particularly beneficial for serialized physical goods, where every unit has a dedicated equipment ID linked to it during the post goods issue. This association is achieved through Reference Objects, which act as a bridge connecting sales items to their respective services. This automation not only reduces errors but also enhances operational efficiency, ensuring that service activities are well-aligned with the relevant product equipment data.

The link between items is created when you establish an item relationship of "One-Time Service for" or "One-Time Service by" between the sales item and the service item in the solution order. This relationship facilitates the effortless transfer of the equipment number, along with its serial number, from the sales item to the service item. It ensures that all related services are comprehensively equipped to proceed.

The below screenshot shows the item relationship being maintained for a one time service in Solution Order Management. The entire process mirrors the process for equipment linkage for service contracts, which you can see here.

The Future of Business, Today: Get Your Access to AI-Assisted Features Thanks to this Limited Promotion Now!  

The 2502 update is a strategic investment that combines advanced AI with powerful enterprise management tools. Whether scaling your business, enhancing productivity, or building sustainable operations, its capabilities help support a more agile and resilient future. 

Sign up for following features through the limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (for all countries, customers, and partners): 

  • The easy filter and smart summarization features in SAP Fiori allow for efficient, natural language filtering of business data and seamless summarization capabilities, enabling users to quickly extract relevant information and jump-start subsequent collaboration.  
  • Facilitate easier understanding and interpretation of financial data with insights from cost center reviews and explanations of fixed-asset depreciation keys. 

 

 

More Information 

To catch up with the full scope of Solution Order Management features from previous releases, plus further deep-dive content on Solution Business related topics, check out my collection blog.

 

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!    

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience – anywhere, anytime.   

   

 

Find All of Our Assets    

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Public Cloud means continuous innovation and continuous learning – new user group to facilitate both
entry.content:
One of the main benefits of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is that you receive continuous innovations without having to perform upgrades on your systems. Different companies have different ways of managing change, and there are lessons to be learned on the best ways to encourage your business users consume new functionality.

To facilitate sharing of best practices regarding SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition usage, we are hosting an online user group that meets every other month where people can share their best practices and we can demo new innovations, either pre- or post-release. Participants can learn from each other and SAP experts, preview new functionality, provide feedback on the latest features, and participate in user research to shape future features.

The Public Cloud Practitioner Forum meets every other month on the third Thursday at 11am Eastern time / 8am Pacific time / 17:00 CET. We welcome customers from any region, as long as you:

  • are live with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • have 25 or more active users
  • want to empower your users with the best possible experience
  • are open to having your end-users participate in occasional user research projects
  • will attend a 1-hour online meeting every other month

If you would like to join, please ask your account team or sign up here:

https://tinyurl.com/public-cloud-prac-forum

(Invitations will be sent within a week of your signup.)

For the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Practitoner Forum team, Peter Spielvogel and Esther Vrieze.

entry.title: Creating a custom report in S/4HANA Cloud for Sales to display physical stock and sales order confir
entry.content:
In the logistical processes within an ERP system, we differentiate between physical (on-hand) stock on available stock. Physical stock is the total amount of inventory physically available in the supplying company’s plant, while available stock in simple terms represents the physical stock minus any quantities reserved for open transactions. The exact available quantity in the S/4HANA Cloud system is calculated using the mechanism of the so-called ATP (Available-to-promise) check. For a detailed overview on this topic, please refer to the following blog post.

With this introduction, we can take away that there are two stock quantities that are relevant when it comes to inventory management – physical quantity and available quantity. Within the S/4HANA Cloud system, these can be seen on material/plant level in a corresponding app – “Display Stock Overview” for the physical quantity and “Monitor Product Availability” for the available quantity. However, what happens when business users would like to see a consolidated view of the stock quantities across all materials in the system. This recently came up as a requirement in one of the projects as the customer previously had custom developed reports in an on-premise legacy system, which was used to display this aggregated data of both these quantities along with other relevant attributes. In this blog post, I would like to detail how a fairly similar result could be achieved using a Custom CDS View report in S/4HANA Cloud.

Step 1: Finding the CDS Views

In our case, this was the most challenging step. It is important to make sure that the CDS View to be used is released for key-user extensibility, such that it can be used for consumption in a Custom CDS View.

In our case, there were only limited CDS Views that fulfilled the exact data we were looking to display. To display the physical quantity, the View we decided on was I_StockQuantityValueByType. For the available quantity unfortunately, there is currently no CDS View which that exactly displays the available quantity details purely on material/plant level. The closest CDS View found was C_SalesDocumentSchedLineDEX_1, which provided the confirmed quantity with respect to the order quantity during the creation of a sales order item. This therefore doesn’t provide the total available quantity information as in the “Monitor Product Availability” app, rather shows whether the material was available in the desired quantity during the time of the ATP check.

Step 2: Creating the Custom CDS View

With the two Views selected above, we proceeded with the Creation of a Custom CDS View. Based on the individual requirements, this can be created as a Standard CDS View, analytical dimension or external API. Choose the following values for “Data Sources”, “Elements” and “Filter”:

 

 

 

 

 

Step 3: Publish the Custom CDS View and validate result

Now, we publish the Custom CDS View and can see how the results turn out.

 

In this excerpt for one example material, we can see the schedule line order and confirmed quantity during the time of the sales order creation. In this case, there was a fully confirmed schedule line, due to which the requested one piece was available and confirmed for the item 40 of sales order 93. Next to this, we see the physical quantity represented in the column “MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit”. As this is on storage location level, we therefore have a total physical quantity of 593 pieces in Plant 2901. As mentioned earlier, due to a lack of an exact CDS View to fetch the overall available quantity on material/plant level, only the sales order item specific schedule line quantities can be displayed. To have the exact available quantity as through the “Monitor Product Availability” app, a feasible approach would be to use the standard API for this to pull this data into a custom application on the BTP and display this as a report there.

Hopefully, this blog post gave an idea on how to use a custom CDS View report to achieve certain aggregations of data using embedded analytics, which have previously been achieved by custom development efforts in legacy systems.

entry.title: RAP On-Stack Developer Extensibility
entry.content:
Problem statement:
How to develop custom extensions for standard ABAP RAP based business objects in S/4HANA Public, Private Cloud, BTP ABAP Environment and On-Premise Platforms?

Solution:
RAP Developer Extensibility is the solution. The topic gives an overview of RAP developer extensibility architecture, extension development artifacts such as BO interfaces, all available extensibility features and how to develop RAP custom extensions such as data model extension, behavior extensions and node extensions for standard ABAP RAP based applications.

I am on of the presenter for this topic at ABAP Conference and int the below youtube recording. This topic covers contented related to extensibility architecture overview, extensibility use cases,  RAP BO Interfaces Concept, How Procurement Project Standard RAP based application is extended with custom extensions and some useful links which can be explored related to RAP Developer Extensibility. 

ABAP Conference recording:  Deep Dive into RAP Developer Extensibility

Slides: https://abapconf.org/abapconfeurope2024/w1w2.html

Check for topic "Deep Dive into RAP Developer Extensibility" where you can find the link to download slides in pdf document.

Use cases covered:

  1. Data Model Extensions 
    • Adding Custom fields to standard CDS views
  2. Behavior and Field related behavior extensions 
    • Adding Dynamic feature control for custom field based on standard field.
    • Adding a new Custom Action
    • Adding Validation for standard field.

  3. Node Extensions
    • Adding a new custom node for a standard RAP BO.
  4. Service Definition Extension

Note:  Demo shown in youtube recording for procurement project application is implemented without using wizards. That means all required extension objects are created manually in ADT.

In ABAP Cloud in the latest version you can also use below ABAP Repository object wizard for generating extension fields to the selected  RAP Business Object.

Below TechEd git code sample exercises give more information about developer extensibility generation using above wizard 
RAP630 - Use ABAP Cloud for developer extensibility where Developer Extensibility is used.
Exercise 3 - Extend the CDS data model using the field extension wizard

You can find out all released RAP BO Interfaces in SAP Business Accelerator Hub in the On stack Extensibility section.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for group reporting - Release 2502 is generally available
entry.content:
We're glad to announce the release of SAP S/4HANA for group reporting 2502 on February 15, 2025, with new and improved features to help you run your corporate close process. This new release introduces several key innovations, significantly enhancing the data collection process through new features for the Group Reporting Data Collection app. The 2502 release also helps you simplify data quality controls with the integration of multiple line and balance validations. Additionally, the analytics review booklet has been improved to include superior commenting capabilities and AI-assisted insights. A new app offering an analytical group view on unreleased accounting data has also been introduced, which streamlines your reporting processes. Furthermore, connectivity to the SAP Green Ledger is now available, facilitating better management of greenhouse gas emissions at consolidated level.

Download our feature deck.

Key innovations in release Group Reporting 2502 include:

  • Launch your controls from within the Group Reporting Data Collection package
  • Easy navigation between Group Reporting Data Collection forms
  • Defining several fiscal years, periods, and versions for one Group Reporting Data Collection form
  • Advanced monitoring and import alerts in Group Reporting Data Collection's mapping app
  • Improved data mapping logs in Group Reporting Data Collection
  • Multiple line validation tasks on non-consolidated data to enhance your efficiency in data quality
  • Multiple balance validation tasks on non-consolidated data to enhance your efficiency in data quality
  • Extending usability in creating manual journals using replace feature
  • Review booklet, improved analytical cell comments and notifications
  • Review booklet, AI-assisted financial business insights for group statements (beta)
  • New app with an analytical group view on accounting data
  • Streamlining sustainability reporting by integrating SAP Green Ledger with Group Reporting
  • New master data capabilities to delete financial statement items 
  • Enhanced user experience to display master data change of financial statement items
  • Tight accounting integration, option to release data from prior periods
  • New master data features including read APIs and consolidation hierarchy management

--
Group Reporting Data Collection: Launch your data collection controls from within the package

With release 2502, we integrate Group Reporting Data Collection (GRDC) package with the data monitor task execution. Now, you can directly run the controls and perform a series of data monitor tasks directly from within the package. This key innovation is part of our roadmap acceleration program, which aims at developing the best Financial Consolidation features in Group Reporting. 

With this innovation, you benefit from an increased efficiency and embedded data quality controls in your data collection process.

In the "Enter Package Data" app of Group Reporting Data Collection:

  • The forms navigation tree is enhanced with new actions
  • You can run group reporting tasks as an action (release of data, annual net-income calculation, and line validations)
  • Display the status of a task directly in the app
  • Access the last log of a task execution from within the app

How does it work? In the "Manage Package" app, you can define data entry steps based on tasks defined in the data monitor. Tasks from the data monitor are integrated with the Manage Packages app, and are then displayed in the Enter Package Data app. When you enter a version, start fiscal year, and period, one step is automatically created and the tasks for this step are populated with the tasks from the data monitor in your SAP S/4HANA system. You can define additional steps and tasks. For each task, you can assign folders, and define read and write document type values centrally.

For more information: Manage Packages.

--
Group Reporting Data Collection: Easy navigation between forms

Also part of our roadmap acceleration program, in 2502, you benefit from an enhanced navigation with a "navigate-to" feature between forms within a package, to provide more guidance when entering data in forms.

You can define links between forms at design time, in the "Manage Forms" app. Single or multiple target forms are supported. When opening the form in the "Enter Package Data" app, the cells that offer navigation are indicated by a triangle. Just open the target form when navigating to it from the source form.

For more information: Create a Shortcut.

--
Defining several fiscal years, periods, and versions for one Group Reporting Data Collection form

Also part of our roadmap acceleration program, with 2502 you can now define several fiscal years, periods, and versions for one form in Group Reporting Data Collection. Use this feature if you need to enter data of different fiscal years and periods in one single form for your management reporting, or display comparative data in a form (the previous month or a comparison version, for instance), which can include formulas that calculate the difference between the current period and the comparative period, year, or version.

This new feature addresses multiple use cases, such as:

  • When working on the current actuals period, you want to see the prior year data of the same period in the form as a display column
  • When working on the current actuals period, you want to see the prior period of the same year in the form as a display column
  • When working on a restatement of the prior year in an extension version, you want to enter data for all periods of the year at the same time, both in periodic or year-to-date mode
  • When working on the financial plan of the next year(s) in a plan version, you want to enter data for fiscal years or for the budget all 12 months of the year at the same time (screenshot below, where months are in column)
  • When working on the current actuals period, you want to see the corresponding budget version in the form as a display column.

For more information: Multiple Periods and Multiple Fiscal Years.

--
Advanced monitoring and import alerts in Group Reporting Data Collection's mapping app

With the advanced monitoring and import alerts in Group Reporting Data Collection, you can now monitor all last job runs in a single view and benefit from new capabilities, including a run status, visual display of errors, warnings, information and successes in micro-charts, and direct access to logs and files. Within the same app, you can also define users to be notified by email depending on the job run status. Key features include:

  • Complete overview of the upload process at one glance
  • Detailed information about the last job runs
  • Enhanced usability of the job list
  • One-click access to logs and files
  • Notifications and alerts

For more information: Advanced Monitoring and Import Alerts.

--
Improved data mapping logs in Group Reporting Data Collection

With 2502, you can leverage improved job processing, traceability log, and mapping log features in the "Data Mapping" app of Group Reporting Data Collection. These enhancements simplify, streamline, and secure the mapping and import process for operations in production environments. They also make processes and logs easier to understand and analyze.

Key improvements include:

  • Enhanced job processing
    – Automatically derive the local currency key from consolidation unit master data during import
    – Define import options in the mapping definition (instead of in the job)
    – Streamline job run header information and move additional information into a separate tab
    – Support .txt extension files as a source

  • Enhanced traceability log
    – Remove the limit of 30 million characters
    – New sheet with job run information such as that displayed in the job run Overview tab
    – New mapping file sheets to the traceability log file
    – Improved sorting and filtering capabilities (for example, removal of merged cells in header rows and change data types, default freeze panes)
    – Display mapping messages before mapping rules and the import messages before output fields
    – Ease the filtering of messages to only display errors and warning

  • Mapping log (screenshot below)
    – Display error and warning messages at the top
    – Display the name of the field and the value that is the origin of the error or warning
    – Remove unnecessary information messages from the mapping log
    – Improve the quality of error messages to provide more helpful guidance to users
    – Display the name of the field and the value that is the origin of the error or warning
    – Remove unnecessary information messages from the mapping log
    – Improve the quality of error messages to provide more helpful guidance to users

For more information: Run Information.

--
Multiple line validation tasks on non-consolidated data to enhance your efficiency in managing data quality control

Line-item validation is a preventive control to help you ensure data quality of non-consolidated data, either entered with Group Reporting Data Collection, or directly integrated from general accounting. It automatically checks if a combination of field values is allowed. A typical line-item validation message would be “Consolidation unit S001 doesn’t allow Profit center PC002”.

In release 2502, we release multiple line-validation tasks on group reporting and unreleased accounting data to improve your efficiency in controlling data quality in different scenarios such as:

  • Staggered data collection process, with submission and validation of different data sets at different dates (such as profit and loss first, balance sheet later)
  • Loading of remote system data into group reporting, running validations as separate tasks, and deciding to correct errors either at the source or in group reporting
  • Smooth and fast group closing process by running quality checks on unreleased accounting data early, right after the accounting posting becomes available, and applying the group reporting quality definitions

--
Multiple balance validation tasks on non-consolidated data to enhance your efficiency in managing data quality control

Balance validation helps you ensure data quality during the period-end close of your group. It validates data completeness, correctness, and compliance of balance sheet and P&L. You can define balance validation rules to check two selected amounts against each other, whether financial amounts or quantities. A typical example would be “Assets equal Liabilities and Equity”.

In release 2502, you benefit from enhanced balance validation task abilities, improving your efficiency in controlling data quality in different scenarios, such as:

  • Staggered data collection process, with submission and validation of different data sets at different dates, such as profit and loss first and balance sheet later, leveraging multiple balance validation tasks
  • Smooth and fast group closing process by running quality checks on unreleased accounting data early, right after the accounting posting becomes available, and applying the group reporting quality definitions to unreleased accounting data
  • Higher efficiency in rule creation when more than three group-by fields are needed
  • Higher usability in rule group assignment to consolidation units and groups leveraging selection object features, such as select by consolidation unit attribute or hierarchy

You can now validate balance selections in multiple tasks and run group reporting rule definitions on unreleased accounting data, group reporting data on the consolidation unit level, and on the consolidation group level.

You can use a new configuration interface to define balance validation tasks, allowing you to create multiple tasks as needed to separate validation rules by different user ownership or steps in the closing process, and assign execution types to tasks to determine the data source (unreleased accounting data, consolidation unit level, or consolidation group level).

A new balance validation infrastructure for financial data is available for group reporting, including new or adapted apps.

The rule definition app is replaced with a new SAP Fiori app for validation rule and group definitions, reusing the accounting balance validation rule definition app in Group Reporting.  Existing group reporting validation rules and methods are migrated to this new app.

Also, you can now use a new SAP Fiori app to assign a validation rule group to a validation task based on versions, periods, and consolidation units or groups. Existing method assignments are migrated to this new app.

Additionally, other new features for balance validation include the use of up to six group-by fields in rules definition, assignment of rule groups to consolidation units and groups, and support for multiple languages for rule description fields.

For more information, check out our new balance validation blog and see how to:

  • Run balance validation and view results
  • Manage balance validation rules and rule groups
  • Assign rule group for balance validation
  • Import/Export validation settings

--
Extending usability in creating manual journals using replace feature

In 2502, you benefit from an enhanced usability of the Post Group Journal Entries app, with a new Replace button to reverse and copy a manual journal entry in draft mode. It sometimes happen that you make a wrong manual journal entry, or need to update it. The Replace feature enables you to do so, while keeping the original entry to ensure audit continuity and completeness.

The Replace action is available on the overview screen of the Post Group Journal Entries app. It combines the Reverse and Copy actions. The reversal document is posted in the background, and a draft of the copied document is prepared, allowing the user to make modifications.

If the reversal is part of a workflow, the system creates a workflow item in the background. The posting then waits for approval (suggestion: “the posting must be approved”). If the new document isn’t part of a workflow, it might be posted before the reversal. If you decide to replace a document that is part of a document bundle, the system replaces the entire bundle.

  1. The original document references the reversal document in the Reversed With field.
  2. The reversal document references the original document in the Reversed Document field.
  3. The corrected document does not have any reference.

For more information: Post Group Journal Entries

--
Review booklet, improved analytical cell comments and notifications

With 2502, you benefit from improved commenting capabilities in the group financial statement review booklet.

Key new features include:

  • Analytical cell-based comments
    – Ability to discuss numbers in certain cell
    – Categorize, tag, filter and edit
    – History of comments and contributions
    – Life cycle status for each comment
    – Comments of all loaded cells
  • Show all loaded comments on a page (screenshot above)
    – Rename “Cell Comment Header”
    – Classify and filter comments

  • Comments of selected cells
    – Add and view comment via context menu
    – Click on comment to see detailed history

In addition, you can use the new "@Mention" feature in your comments (screenshot below). Your mentioned colleagues are notified about your comment in the in-app notification and via email.

For more information: watch our video. 

--
Review booklet, AI-assisted financial business insights for group statements (beta)

Part of SAP's vision for Business AI, we deliver a new AI-assisted feature for financial business insights for group financial statement review booklet. This new beta feature embeds generative AI in the Review Booklet to help you distill insights on group performance, identify outliers in large quantities of data, and summarize comments in a fraction of the time.

Key capabilities include:

  • Analyze and summarize tabular data on a review booklet page
  • Identify and evaluate relevant business situations
  • Highlight findings within large datasets
  • Create a management summary of group-level financial data
  • Include comments and calculate KPIs

You can experience generative AI in the Review Booklet and influence our Group Reporting business AI roadmap by applying to our beta program.

--
New app with an analytical group view on accounting data

From the perspective of the integration of consolidation with general accounting, in Group Reporting 2502, we introduce a new application with an analytical group view on accounting data. With this new app you can analyze data in a group view directly on accounting data for the following scenarios:

  • Anytime reporting on accounting data in a group view reflecting the latest posting documents from accounting. You can combine the reporting with prediction and transactional consolidation ledgers from accounting to offer forward-looking consolidated management views based on the latest accounting data.
  • Analysis reporting for companies submitting their data to group reporting. For example, checking for unreleased data and analyzing the impact on reports like P&L of their next data release to group reporting.

These early insights can be valuable for accessing the latest accounting data. This way, you can make informed decisions based on current accounting information in the group language.

Key features:

  • Group view on latest accounting material
  • Reflects all accounting journals
  • Translated @spot rate of the transaction
  • Presented in your group definitions
  • Filter parameters: consolidation group, consolidation unit, GR posting periods, among others
  • Reporting row structures driven by FS item hierarchies
  • Reporting rule definitions and reporting item hierarchies (for example Cash Flow Statement)
  • Predefined column structures for period, quarter, year comparison

For more information: Group View on Accounting Review Booklet

--
Streamlining sustainability reporting by integrating SAP Green Ledger with SAP Group Reporting

With release 2502, you can further integrate your greenhouse gas emission reporting process on group level. You can leverage new features to collect greenhouse gas information, eliminate intercompany transactions both in quantity (for example tons of carbon emissions) and amount, and produce a consolidated sustainability reporting.

In addition to the five specific fields for greenhouse gas (GHG) emission reporting already supported within group reporting – GHG Scope, GHG Category, GHG Scope 2 Calculation Method, GHG Scope 2 Contractual Instrument and Sustainability Mode of Transport – you can now use four new fields:

  • GHG Data Quality Characteristic
  • GHG Energy Classification
  • GHG Energy Mix
  • GHG Energy Sourcing Type

Furthermore, with release 2502, we help you streamline your sustainability reporting by integrating SAP Green Ledger with SAP Group Reporting. This integration is enabled by using the data mapping apps within SAP Group Reporting Data Collection.

Given the increasing importance of sustainability reporting, driven by investor and regulatory scrutiny, you may face challenges in navigating complex regulations like IFRS S2 and require robust data management solutions.

SAP Green Ledger captures granular environmental data, while SAP Group Reporting is our platform for consolidated financial and environmental reporting. Today, we integrate these systems to provide comprehensive sustainability insights.

The data mapping apps within SAP Group Reporting Data Collection allow you to:

  • Connect SAP Green Ledger and SAP Group Reporting by enabling the ETL (extraction, transformation, and load) of environmental data from SAP Green Ledger into the SAP Group Reporting consolidation table (ACDOCU)
  • Seamlessly configure an OData service within SAP Green Ledger to expose the necessary data
  • Create data mapping definitions using mapping files to define rules for transforming data from SAP Green Ledger for the consolidation table (see screenshot below)
  • Automate the import process and generate the required reports

By leveraging data mapping apps to integrate SAP Green Ledger with SAP Group Reporting, you can:

  • Streamline sustainability reporting processes
  • Meet regulatory requirements like IFRS S2
  • Gain valuable insights into your environmental impact
  • Make more informed business decisions

This integration empowers your organization to move beyond basic compliance and use sustainability data to drive strategic initiatives, creating a more sustainable future.

For more information:

  • Streamlining Sustainability Reporting: Integrating SAP Green Ledger with SAP Group Reporting
  • Group Reporting Fields for Greenhouse Gas Emissions
  • Group-level Green Ledger - managing greenhouse gas emissions for group reporting

--
New master data capabilities to delete financial statement items 

In release 2502, you benefit from improved usability in managing financial statement items. A new feature in the "Define Financial Statements" app allows you to delete financial statement items. This feature is secured by a "Check Where Used" capability that prevents the deletion of financial statement items that are still in use.

You can delete financial statement items on both the overview and details screens of the "Define Financial Statement Items" app. This applies to customer-defined financial statement items as well as those delivered with SAP content.

For data and master data security reasons, you may only delete financial statement items that are no longer in use and that you are authorized to delete. If either of these prerequisites is violated, an error message explaining the reason why the financial statement item could not be deleted will be displayed.

If you cannot delete a financial statement item because it is still in use, you can utilize the "Check Where Used" functionality within the "Define Financial Statement Items" app. This will provide information about the usage of the financial statement item. After removing all usages of a financial statement item, you can then proceed with its deletion.

For more information: Delete FS Items in the 'Define FS Items' App.

--
Enhanced user experience to display master data change of financial statement items

Tracking master data changes is crucial for several reasons. Firstly, it enhances operational efficiency by maintaining a single source of truth, reducing the risk of errors caused by data inconsistencies. Tracking changes aids in compliance and risk management, helping organizations meet regulatory requirements and mitigate risks related to data breaches or inaccuracies. Moreover, it is particularly important during audits, as auditors need to verify the accuracy and consistency of data over time. Overall, a clear, precise, and usable app to display master data changes enhances your experience by ensuring consistent and accurate information.

Part of 2502 release, we introduce the ability to display master data change log information in the “Define Financial Statement Items” app, offering unprecedented access to detailed change logs of financial statement items.

One of the key aspects of the new "Show Change Log" feature is its user-friendly interface. Unlike the technical backend transaction, which required knowledge of database table names, the new integrated access is designed with the end-user in mind. This means that users can now easily access and understand changes made to financial statement items without needing technical expertise.

The change log information is meticulously organized for your enhanced readability. Changes are grouped by timestamp, user, and type of change, making it straightforward to track modifications. The log includes all master data attributes, such as time- and version-independent attributes, time- and version-dependent attributes, and language-dependent attributes. This comprehensive coverage ensures that users have a complete view of all changes made to financial statement items.

Filtering options further enhance the usability of this feature. You can filter the displayed change log information by various dimensions, including date, username, language, and attribute. This allows for targeted analysis and quick identification of specific changes. Additionally, you can export the change log information.

For public cloud customers, this feature marks the first time you have access to the master data change log of financial statement items. This is a significant step forward in providing comprehensive and accessible financial management tools to a broader audience.

The "Define Financial Statement Items" app's new feature for displaying master data change log information is a powerful tool that enhances transparency, accountability, and usability in financial statement management. By providing a user-friendly interface, comprehensive attribute coverage, and robust filtering options, this feature empowers you to efficiently track and understand changes in master data.

More information: Display Master Data Change Log for FS Item.

--
Tight accounting integration, option to release data from prior periods

In the 2502 release, you now have the option to release data from prior periods in the “Release of Universal Documents for Consolidation” app. This new feature increases efficiency by integrating late accounting data from previous periods, such as postings made after the previous period's closure in group reporting (“Delta”). Additionally, it aids in activating group reporting on productive accounting environments with initial year-to-date data integration (“Complete Data”).

Use the “Delta” mode to integrate late accounting data. When the group close has commenced, late postings in general accounting may occur. This data from prior periods, which may already be closed, needs processing. You can achieve this seamlessly using the new option to release data from prior periods. This feature eliminates the need to reopen and reprocess the “Release Universal Journal” task in the current period mode, as well as all subsequent consolidation tasks in respective periods. The prior periods mode option allows you to process accounting data from closed periods and transfer the results into the current execution period.

Use the “Complete Data” mode when you want to activate group reporting on a productive accounting environment, transferring opening balances. This mode is particularly relevant for creating a starting point for a consolidation unit in group reporting.

You can use the new option to release data from prior periods on-demand via your “Data Monitor,” or schedule a job using the “Schedule Jobs for Consolidation Tasks” app.

For more information: Release Universal Journal: Support of Prior Periods Mode.

--
New master data features including read APIs and consolidation hierarchy management

With release 2502, you also benefit from additional new features that improve your master data management.

  • New API for reading group reporting transactional data with a reporting item – Increases flexibility in extracting data from group reporting with a reporting item that gives access to advanced reports such as the cash flow statement in an API call and improves performance compared to the old API. More information: Consolidation Global Parameter.

  • Display unassigned master data values feature in the "Manage Consolidation Hierarchies" app – You can now display master data values which are not (yet) assigned to a consolidation hierarchy in the “Manage Consolidation Hierarchies” app. This feature is available for all consolidation hierarchy types except “Consolidation Posting Level” hierarchies. 
    More information: Manage Consolidation Hierarchies.

  • Aggregation of additional master data fields during Activity Based Consolidation of Investments (ABCOI) – As of release 2502 a new column “Aggregate on Activity-Based COI” has been introduced to the additional master data field configuration. This provides flexibility in defining the level of granularity of ABCOI.
    More information: Aggregate Additional Master Data Fields During Activity-Based Consolidation of Investments.

  • Support financial statement item and consolidation unit attribute special version assignment to Group Reporting Preparation Ledger (GRPL) – As of release 2502 you can assign a “Consolidation Unit Attribute Version” as optional attribute to a GRPL configuration. Together with the fiscal year and period of an accounting posting, these GRPL attributes are used to identify time- and version-dependent FS item or consolidation unit attribute values which are used in the precondition of a substitution rule in the business context “GRPL core fields” or “GRPL sub-assignments to FS item” for accounting postings.
    More information: Configuration for Group Reporting Preparation Ledger.

  • Support of additional consolidation hierarchy types and other changes in the "Manage Consolidation Hierarchies" app – As of release 2502, the following additional hierarchy types are supported in the “Manage Consolidation Hierarchies” app: Consolidation Billing Document Type, Consolidation Distribution Channel, Consolidation Division, Consolidation Industry, Consolidation Plant, Consolidation Product Sold Group, Consolidation Sales District.
    More information: Manage Consolidation Hierarchies.

  • Import Consolidation Master Data app now supports all master data types, and further improvements –Before release 2502 there were two apps for consolidation master data import. With 2502, there is only a single app: all master data types, i.e. consolidation units, consolidation groups, consolidation group structures, FS items, and all additional master data fields, which are configured as master data enabled in the additional master data field configuration, can now be imported via the “Import Consolidation Master Data” app.
    More information: Import Consolidation Master Data.

  • Partner Fund available as additional industry scenario field for public sector – With release 2502, an additional industry specific field for public sector is available in Group Reporting five industry specific fields for public sector were made available in group reporting: Partner Fund. This field complements the already existing ones dedicated to the public sector: Financial Management Area, Fund, Grant, Cash Origin Chart of Accounts, Cash Origin Account.
    More information: Additional Field for Public Sector

--
For more information

  • Group reporting 2502 feature deck
  • Group reporting 2502 help portal
  • Recap of group reporting new features
  • Group reporting community
  • Group reporting customer influence platform

entry.title: Environment, Health, and Safety in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
With this blog I will walk you through my selected key highlights for Environment, Helth and Saftey in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Pubic Edition 2502:

  • New Application for Managing the Investigation Process,
  • Integration with Learning Management Systems 
  • Electronic Submission of Recordable Injury or Illness Cases to OSHA 
  • Automatic Generation of Emissions Declarations
  • Create and Manage Requests for Chemical Approval

In case you do not need all the information below and just briefly want to get an overview of the release highlights, please watch this video with the top three key innovations:

Video #1: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 for Environment, Health, and Safety

In case your EHS team wants to get better transparency into compliance performance using the new Compliance Overview dashboard in SAP Analytics Cloud, please kindly explore the blog post Compliance Performance Visibility: Exploring the New Compliance Overview Dashboard.

New Application for Managing the Investigation Process

Business Background

Investigations help in identifying the underlying causes of accidents or incidents, whether they are related to human factors, equipment failure, procedural errors, or environmental conditions. This thorough analysis is essential to develop effective corrective and preventive actions.

In the same way, properly managing investigations ensures compliance with legal requirements and regulatory standards. It demonstrates an organization's commitment to investigating incidents thoroughly and taking necessary actions to prevent similar incidents in the future.

Key Feature

In the previous release, this feature was available as an optional feature. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502, it is generally available and doesn’t require any activation.

It is enabling the investigation leaders to manage the investigation process and document the investigation result in a separate application and empowering expert users to manage the investigation result without access to the incident. The user need only read access to the incidents.

It is allowing investigation team to Plan the investigation process, Collect evidence, Analyze root causes and make recommendations to prevent the future incidents. For more information, see Manage Investigations and following Demo.

 


Video #2: DEMO of Manage Investigations App

Integration with Learning Management Systems

Business Background

Previously, when an incident investigation warranted a learning assignment, the process involved several manual steps within the Learning Management System. First, the Incident Manager had to notify the HR or Line Manager of the relevant Learning Assignee. Then, the Line Manager would assign the learning tasks in the Learning Management System for the Assignee. Finally, any changes to the learning task status had to be manually communicated back to the Incident Manager.

With this new release, you will be able to assign content from an integrated learning management system (LMS) to employees who need to fulfill certain training, qualification, or certification requirements.

Key Feature

In the new Task Definition: Learning Task app (F8398), you can create a task for an employee to complete a learning in an integrated learning management system. You can select a learning from a list of items that are available and active in the LMS and that match your predefined configuration settings for learning item types.

This feature currently supports integration with the SAP SuccessFactors Learning solution.

For more information, check SAP Help Portal and below Demo.


Video#3: DEMO of Integration with Learning Management System

Implementation Details

To set up the integration with SAP SuccessFactors Learning, you'll need the following:

  • A tenant on the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) withSAP Integration Suite enabled.
  • An SAP SuccessFactors Learning system connected to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
  • A business user with a business role in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. The business role must contain the business catalogSAP_CORE_BC_COM (Communication Management), for example, the SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR (Administrator) business role.

For more information on the necessary setup and configuration activities, see Setting Up Integration with SAP SuccessFactors Learning.

Electronic Submission of Recordable Injury or Illness Cases to OSHA 

Business Background

OSHA stands for the Occupational Safety and Health Administration. It's a federal agency in the United States under the Department of Labor responsible for ensuring safe and healthful working conditions for workers by setting and enforcing standards and by providing training, outreach, education, and assistance.

This feature enables to submit injury and illness data to OSHA in the USA electronically and ensure compliance with OSHA's electronic reporting requirements.

Prerequisites

To submit injury and illness data to the OSHA electronically, the following prerequisites must be fulfilled:

  • You have completed the necessary setup and configuration activities. For more information, see the following sections:
    • Setting Up Electronic Submission to OSHA (USA)
    • Configuration for Regulation-Based Reporting
  • You have ensured that the following information is available in the incident record of each case that you want to report:
    • Injured person
    • Incident location 

Note:

The Establishment ID and Establishment Name identifiers must be assigned to the incident location in the Manage Locations app.

  • The OSHA (USA) regulation has been assigned to the incident.
  • On the Reports tab of the incident record, an OSHA 301 form has been added for each injured person, with the Reporting Required field set to Yes.

Key Feature

To report work-related injury and illness data electronically, carry out the following steps:

  1. Create an electronic submission.
    In the Manage Electronic Submissions app (F8152), create a record and specify the location (establishment) for which you are reporting, the applicable regulation, and the relevant reporting period.
  2. Generate the required data.
    Generate the data required to be submitted electronically according to the selected regulation. The system extracts the relevant data and generates the following reports:
    OSHA 301 (Injury and Illness Incident Report)
    OSHA 300 (Log of Work-Related Injuries and Illnesses)
    OSHA 300A (Summary of Work-Related Injuries and Illnesses)
  3. Send the data.
    Send the relevant data to OSHA for validation. The system displays the web service response detailing the status of each report. In case of errors, you can correct the data and resend it.
  4. Complete the electronic submission.
    When all required injury and illness data has been sent successfully, complete the electronic submission, which finalises the reporting process.

For more information, check Electronic Submission of Injury and Illness Data to OSHA (USA) and below Demo.


Video#4: Demo Electronic Submission of Injury and Illness Data to OSHA

Automatic Generation of Emissions Declarations

Business Background

As an Environmental Manager, you can schedule the periodic generation of emissions declarations within the Manage Compliance Scenario app, In this release, during the creation of Emissions Declaration Scenario, under the Basic Information tab, there's a new section called Emissions Declaration Settings available.

With this setting, you can set up the scenario so that the system automatically generates an emissions declaration based on the specified frequency.

Key Feature

When you open or create an emissions declaration scenario, under the Basic Information tab, there's a new section called Emissions Declaration Settings. This section allows you to set up the scenario so that the system automatically generates an emissions declaration once every chosen reporting period.

You can schedule an emissions declaration for a future reporting period, and you can also schedule one to be generated for a past reporting period with the start date set as early as the current date. Every change to the emissions declaration settings is reflected across all existing scenario revisions, and when you delete a compliance scenario, all the scheduling data is deleted along with it.

If you have pre-existing scenarios of the Emissions Declaration type in your system prior to the current release, this feature won't be automatically applied. To enable this function for such scenarios, you can select the Set Up Automatic Generation button to the right of the Emissions Declaration Settings section, and then the section will become available for editing.

Check Automatic Generation of Emissions Declarations and below Demo.


Video#5: Demo Automatic Generation of Emissions Declarations.

Implementation Details

Authorizations

To access the Manage Compliance Scenario app to schedule emissions declarations, your user must have the following business catalog assigned: SAP_BR_EHS_ENV_MGMT (Environmental Manager).

Configuration

In your configuration environment, you can manage the menu options available in the Emissions Declaration Settings section using the following configuration activities:

  • Specify Reporting Period Types
  • Specify Recurrence Types for the Environmental Scheduler
  • Specify Contexts for the Environmental Scheduler

The following BAdIs are also available for developer extensibility:

  • Specify Recurrence Types for the Environmental Scheduler
  • Specify Contexts for the Environmental Scheduler
  • Schedule Jobs for Automatic Execution
  • Specify Reporting Period Types

For more information on configuration, see Configuration for Environment Management.

Create and Manage Requests for Chemical Approval

Business Background

In today's dynamic and complex industrial landscape, companies are constantly innovating and expanding their operations to stay competitive. This often involves the introduction of new chemicals into manufacturing processes, laboratory experiments, and other industrial applications. While innovation is essential for growth, it also poses significant risks to worker safety and the environment.

Companies across various sectors, from manufacturing and pharmaceuticals to agriculture and research, frequently deal with a wide array of chemicals. These chemicals can range from benign substances to highly hazardous materials. The proper management of these chemicals is not just a matter of compliance with regulations, but also a critical component of sustainable and responsible business operations.

Key Feature


Figure#1: Create and Manage Requests for Chemical Approval

With the new release, you can create requests for the approval of a new chemical in your company as well as display and continue to work on existing requests as shown in above figure#1.

You can ensure that all safety and environmental considerations associated with chemical usage are thoroughly evaluated. This feature also helps prevent incidents by conducting expert safety assessments and involving the appropriate responsible personnel before any chemical is utilized within your organization.

This feature provides a work view which contains the compliance relevant information that is available for a given raw material or unpackaged product. This information is needed as part of a request for chemical approval. The work view is sectioned and grouped identically to chapters 1 - 9 of the safety data sheet.

For more information, please check Create and Manage Requests for Chemical Approval.

Quick Links

SAP Help Portal:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/323bd3f1dc8248bc8647c62f1baa6a3a/3e0035755e414da09e2b57804d914b9d.html

What’s new:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/350270373b444800872c3bd19b4314c0.html

Where to Find More Information

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

For more information about Environment, Health, and Safety, please see the Link Collection Blog.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn .

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!

From Jan 21 to Jan 24, we hosted a series of 21 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a 50-minute-long recording and a presentation for Environment, Health, and Safety as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

The Future of Business, Today: Get Your Access to AI-Assisted Features Thanks to this Limited Promotion Now! 

The 2502 update is a strategic investment that combines advanced AI with powerful enterprise management tools. Whether scaling your business, enhancing productivity, or building sustainable operations, its capabilities help support a more agile and resilient future.

Sign up for following features through the limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (for all countries, customers, and partners):

  • The easy filter and smart summarization features in SAP Fiori allow for efficient, natural language filtering of business data and seamless summarization capabilities, enabling users to quickly extract relevant information and jump-start subsequent collaboration. 
  • Facilitate easier understanding and interpretation of financial data with insights from cost center reviews and explanations of fixed-asset depreciation keys.

Resource Map
